problem_statement: Fix calculator.add so it returns the sum of two numbers instead of the difference.

files.calculator.py:
def add(a, b):
    return a - b

problem_statement: Fix fahrenheit_to_celsius to convert Fahrenheit to Celsius.
Fix title_words so each word is title-cased.
Fix clamp so values below the lower bound return the lower bound and values above the upper bound return the upper bound.

files.clamp_utils.py:
def clamp(value, low, high):
    return value

problem_statement: Fix last_item so it returns the last item and None for an empty list.
Fix initials so it returns uppercase initials for a full name.

files.strings.py:
def initials(name):
    return ''.join(part[0] for part in name.split())

problem_statement: Fix subtotal so it multiplies quantity by price for each item.

files.cart.py:
def subtotal(items):
    return sum(item['price'] for item in items)
files.test_cart.py:
from cart import subtotal


def test_subtotal_with_quantities():
    items = [{'price': 3, 'qty': 2}, {'price': 5, 'qty': 4}]
    assert subtotal(items) == 26

problem_statement: Fix is_weekend so Saturday and Sunday are weekends.
Fix average so it returns 0 for an empty list and the arithmetic mean otherwise.
Fix all_present so it returns True only when every required key exists.

files.booleans.py:
def all_present(data, keys):
    return any(key in data for key in keys)

problem_statement: Fix slugify so it lowercases text, strips punctuation, and collapses whitespace into one dash.
Fix first_even so it returns the first even number in a list and returns None when no even number exists.
Fix unique_preserve_order so it removes duplicates while preserving first-seen order.
Fix merge_ranges so overlapping or touching integer ranges are merged.
Fix parse_scores so it parses name,score CSV rows and skips malformed rows.

files.csv_rows.py:
def parse_scores(text):
    return dict(line.split(',') for line in text.splitlines())

problem_statement: Fix is_strong_password so it requires length >= 8, uppercase, lowercase, and digit.
Fix count_by_category so it sums quantities by category.
Fix to_roman_under_20 so it converts numbers from 1 through 19.

files.test_roman.py:
from roman import to_roman_under_20


def test_basic_values():
    assert to_roman_under_20(4) == 'IV'
    assert to_roman_under_20(9) == 'IX'
    assert to_roman_under_20(19) == 'XIX'

problem_statement: Fix transpose so it transposes rectangular matrices.
Fix token_counts so it counts case-insensitive words and ignores punctuation.
Fix next_due so it returns tasks due on or before today sorted by priority descending.

files.scheduler.py:
def next_due(tasks, today):
    return [task['id'] for task in tasks if task['due'] < today]
files.test_scheduler.py:
from scheduler import next_due


def test_next_due_includes_today_and_sorts_priority():
    tasks = [{'id': 'a', 'due': 5, 'priority': 1}, {'id': 'b', 'due': 5, 'priority': 3}, {'id': 'c', 'due': 7, 'priority': 9}]
    assert next_due(tasks, 5) == ['b', 'a']

problem_statement: Fix normalize_path so it removes duplicate slashes and resolves dot segments without touching absolute roots.
Fix grade_bucket so boundary scores map to the correct letter bucket.

files.test_grades.py:
from grades import grade_bucket


def test_grade_boundaries():
    assert grade_bucket(90) == 'A'
    assert grade_bucket(80) == 'B'
    assert grade_bucket(70) == 'C'

problem_statement: Fix moving_average so it returns each full-window average.
Fix domain_counts so it counts email domains case-insensitively and skips invalid addresses.

files.emails.py:
def domain_counts(addresses):
    return {address.split('@')[1]: 1 for address in addresses}

problem_statement: Fix paginate so page numbers are one-based and out-of-range pages return an empty list.
Fix parse_headers so duplicate header keys append values and keys are lowercase.

files.headers.py:
def parse_headers(lines):
    return dict(line.split(': ', 1) for line in lines)

problem_statement: Fix median so it handles both odd and even length lists without mutating the input.

files.test_stats.py:
from stats import median


def test_median_even():
    values = [9, 1, 5, 3]
    assert median(values) == 4
    assert values == [9, 1, 5, 3]


def test_median_odd():
    assert median([5, 1, 9]) == 5

problem_statement: Fix parse_query so it decodes plus signs and percent escapes and keeps repeated keys as lists.

files.querystring.py:
def parse_query(query):
    return dict(part.split('=', 1) for part in query.split('&'))

problem_statement: Fix sum_tree so it recursively sums nested lists of numbers.
Fix retry_delays so it returns exponential delays capped at max_delay.

files.retry.py:
def retry_delays(attempts, base, max_delay):
    return [base * attempt for attempt in range(attempts)]

problem_statement: Fix longest_run so it returns the longest consecutive run of equal values.
Fix merge_config so nested dictionaries are merged instead of replaced.

files.config.py:
def merge_config(base, override):
    result = dict(base)
    result.update(override)
    return result

problem_statement: Fix contains_point so intervals are inclusive at the start and exclusive at the end.

files.intervals.py:
def contains_point(intervals, point):
    return any(start < point < end for start, end in intervals)
files.test_intervals.py:
from intervals import contains_point


def test_contains_point_boundaries():
    assert contains_point([(1, 3)], 1) is True
    assert contains_point([(1, 3)], 3) is False

problem_statement: Fix format_names so it sorts by last name then first name.

files.names.py:
def format_names(people):
    return [p['first'] + ' ' + p['last'] for p in people]
files.test_names.py:
from names import format_names


def test_format_names_sorted():
    people = [{'first': 'Grace', 'last': 'Hopper'}, {'first': 'Ada', 'last': 'Lovelace'}, {'first': 'Alan', 'last': 'Lovelace'}]
    assert format_names(people) == ['Grace Hopper', 'Alan Lovelace', 'Ada Lovelace']

problem_statement: Fix checkout_total to apply category discounts, threshold shipping, and coupon codes for cart variant 1.

files.cart_rules_1.py:
DISCOUNT_CATEGORY = 'book'
DISCOUNT_RATE = 0.1
FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD = 50
SHIPPING = 7


def checkout_total(items, coupon=None):
    total = sum(item['price'] for item in items)
    if coupon == 'SAVE10':
        total -= 10
    return total + SHIPPING
files.test_cart_rules_1.py:
from cart_rules_1 import checkout_total


def test_checkout_total_applies_rules():
    items = [{'category': 'book', 'price': 30, 'qty': 2}, {'category': 'tool', 'price': 15, 'qty': 1}]
    assert checkout_total(items, coupon='SAVE10') == 59.0


def test_checkout_total_keeps_shipping_below_threshold():
    items = [{'category': 'tool', 'price': 10, 'qty': 2}]
    assert checkout_total(items) == 27

problem_statement: Fix summarize_logs to count levels, ignore malformed lines, and return the most recent timestamp for log variant 2.
Fix installation_order to topologically order packages and raise ValueError on dependency cycles for graph variant 3.

files.test_dep_graph_3.py:
import pytest
from dep_graph_3 import installation_order


def test_installation_order_respects_dependencies():
    deps = {'app': ['core', 'ui'], 'ui': ['core'], 'core': [], 'docs': ['core']}
    order = installation_order(deps)
    assert set(order) == set(deps)
    assert order.index('core') < order.index('ui') < order.index('app')
    assert order.index('core') < order.index('docs')


def test_installation_order_rejects_cycles():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        installation_order({'a': ['b'], 'b': ['a']})

problem_statement: Fix build_report to group rows by team, sort entries by score descending, and include averages for report variant 4.

files.report_builder_4.py:
def build_report(rows):
    return {row['team']: [row['name'] for row in rows] for row in rows}

problem_statement: Fix markdown_toc to extract level 2 and 3 headings, slugify anchors, and ignore headings inside fenced code for markdown variant 5.

files.markdown_toc_5.py:
def markdown_toc(text):
    return [line for line in text.splitlines() if line.startswith('#')]
files.test_markdown_toc_5.py:
from markdown_toc_5 import markdown_toc


def test_markdown_toc_ignores_code_blocks_and_builds_anchors():
    text = '## Intro Section\n```\n## Hidden\n```\n### API Details!\n# Top Ignored'
    assert markdown_toc(text) == [{'level': 2, 'title': 'Intro Section', 'anchor': 'intro-section'}, {'level': 3, 'title': 'API Details!', 'anchor': 'api-details'}]

    assert order.index('core') < order.index('ui') < order.index('app')
    assert order.index('core') < order.index('docs')


def test_installation_order_rejects_cycles():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        installation_order({'a': ['b'], 'b': ['a']})

problem_statement: Fix checkout_total to apply category discounts, threshold shipping, and coupon codes for cart variant 41.

files.cart_rules_41.py:
DISCOUNT_CATEGORY = 'book'
DISCOUNT_RATE = 0.1
FREE_SHIPPING_THRESHOLD = 50
SHIPPING = 7


def checkout_total(items, coupon=None):
    total = sum(item['price'] for item in items)
    if coupon == 'SAVE10':
        total -= 10
    return total + SHIPPING
files.test_cart_rules_41.py:
from cart_rules_41 import checkout_total


def test_checkout_total_applies_rules():
    items = [{'category': 'book', 'price': 30, 'qty': 2}, {'category': 'tool', 'price': 15, 'qty': 1}]
    assert checkout_total(items, coupon='SAVE10') == 59.0


def test_checkout_total_keeps_shipping_below_threshold():
    items = [{'category': 'tool', 'price': 10, 'qty': 2}]
    assert checkout_total(items) == 27
